description: Rewrite the F1 fusion miner to use general connected subgraph enumeration over DFG RAW edges, replacing the incorrect adjacent-pair chain scanning. The goal is instruction set extension design — discovering multi-instruction data-flow patterns that could become new custom instructions.
goal: Replace the adjacent-pair chain miner with a subgraph-based miner that discovers all connected DFG subgraphs, normalizes them by topology and register roles, aggregates across basic blocks, and outputs the new schema for F2 scoring.
architecture: Three new/rewritten modules: subgraph.py (enumeration algorithm), pattern.py (normalization + dedup model), miner.py (orchestration). F2 modules (constraints.py, scorer.py) adapted for subgraph input. CLI updated with --max-nodes flag.

tasks.title: Create subgraph.py: connected subgraph enumeration
tasks.description: Implement the seed+BFS expansion algorithm to enumerate all connected subgraphs up to MAX_NODES from a DFG. Pure algorithm module with no fusion-specific logic.
steps: Create tools/fusion/subgraph.py with enumerate_subgraphs(nodes, edges, max_nodes) function
Implement seed initialization: create a 1-node subgraph for each DFG node
Implement BFS expansion: for each subgraph S, find neighbor nodes connected by RAW edges to any node in S
Accept expansion if |S| + 1 <= max_nodes and the result is connected
Track which edges belong to each subgraph (subset of original DFG edges)
Return list of subgraphs, each as a tuple of (frozenset of node indices, list of internal edges)
Add deduplication: use frozenset(node_indices) to avoid enumerating the same subgraph twice
validation_criteria: enumerate_subgraphs returns all 1-node subgraphs for any DFG
For a 2-node DFG with one edge, returns both 1-node and 1 two-node subgraph
For float_chain_2.json fixture (2 nodes, 1 edge), returns 3 subgraphs total (2 singletons + 1 pair)
No duplicate subgraphs (verified by frozenset equality)
Subgraph edges are a subset of the original DFG edges
MAX_NODES=1 returns only singleton subgraphs
MAX_NODES=0 or negative raises ValueError

tasks.title: Create test_subgraph.py with fixtures
tasks.description: Write unit tests for the subgraph enumeration module, including new test fixtures for non-adjacent edge DFGs that represent real workload patterns.
steps: Create tools/fusion/tests/test_subgraph.py with TestEnumerateSubgraphs class
Write test for empty DFG (returns empty list)
Write test for single-node DFG (returns one singleton)
Write test for 2-node DFG with edge (returns 3 subgraphs)
Write test for 3-node chain DFG (returns 7 subgraphs: 3 singletons + 3 pairs + 1 triple)
Write test for fan-in DFG (2 sources → 1 consumer) to verify non-adjacent patterns
Write test that MAX_NODES limits output correctly
Create tools/fusion/tests/fixtures/fan_in_3node.json: two flw → one fmadd.s (non-adjacent edges)
Create tools/fusion/tests/fixtures/fan_out_3node.json: one flw → two fmadd.s (fan-out pattern)
Create tools/fusion/tests/fixtures/disconnected.json: two isolated nodes with no edges
validation_criteria: All tests pass with pytest
Fan-in fixture correctly models non-adjacent RAW edges (src=0 dst=2 and src=1 dst=2)
Disconnected fixture has two nodes with no edges between them
Tests cover: empty, singleton, chain, fan-in, fan-out, disconnected, max_nodes limit

tasks.title: Rewrite pattern.py: SubgraphPattern model + normalization
tasks.description: Replace the linear-chain Pattern dataclass with a SubgraphPattern model that captures DAG topology, normalized edge roles, and topological layer structure. Implement normalization from concrete subgraph instances.
steps: Define SubgraphPattern dataclass with fields: opcodes, register_class, size, topology, edges, template_key
Implement normalize_subgraph(subgraph_nodes, subgraph_edges, dfg_nodes, registry) -> SubgraphPattern
In normalize_subgraph: resolve each node's operands via registry.get_flow().resolve()
Determine register_class from the majority class of subgraph nodes
For each edge in the subgraph: find src_role via _find_role(register, src_resolved.dst_regs, src_flow.dst_regs) and dst_role via _find_role(register, dst_resolved.src_regs, dst_flow.src_regs)
Compute topological layers: BFS from roots (nodes with no in-edges in subgraph), assign layer numbers
Within each layer, sort opcodes lexicographically for stability
Build template_key as (tuple(topology), tuple(normalized_edge_tuples), register_class)
Keep existing classify_register() and is_control_flow() functions unchanged
Keep existing _find_role() helper (reuse for subgraph edges)
validation_criteria: SubgraphPattern has opcodes, register_class, size, topology, edges, and template_key fields
template_key is hashable (tuple of tuples)
normalize_subgraph with float_chain_2.json nodes produces SubgraphPattern with 2 opcodes and 1 edge role pair
Two subgraphs with same structure but different register names produce identical template_keys
Topology is a list of lists (layers), not a flat list
classify_register and is_control_flow still exported and working

tasks.title: Create test_pattern.py: normalization + dedup tests
tasks.description: Write unit tests for SubgraphPattern normalization and template_key deduplication.
steps: Create tools/fusion/tests/test_pattern.py with TestSubgraphPattern class
Write test for 2-node subgraph normalization (float_chain_2 pattern)
Write test that two structurally identical subgraphs with different concrete registers produce same template_key
Write test that different edge structures produce different template_keys (fan-in vs chain)
Write test for topological layer computation: fan-in has 2 nodes in layer 0 and 1 in layer 1
Write test for register class determination from node majority
Write test for edge role resolution: flw.frd → fmadd.s.frs3
validation_criteria: All tests pass with pytest
Template key correctly distinguishes fan-in from chain patterns
Topological layers are stable (same input always produces same output)
Edge roles are correctly resolved from concrete register names to role names

tasks.title: Rewrite miner.py: subgraph-based orchestration
tasks.description: Replace the chain-based miner with subgraph-based orchestration: load DFGs → enumerate subgraphs → normalize → aggregate across BBs → write new schema JSON.
steps: Replace enumerate_chains with a call to subgraph.enumerate_subgraphs
Rewrite aggregate_patterns: for each DFG, enumerate subgraphs, normalize each, group by template_key
For each normalized subgraph, look up BBV frequency from bbv_map using vaddr
Accumulate occurrence_count and total_frequency per template_key across BBs
Build pattern output dicts with new schema fields: opcodes, register_class, size, topology, edges, occurrence_count, total_frequency, source_bbs, examples
Store one concrete example per source BB (mnemonic, operands, index for each node in the subgraph)
Sort patterns by total_frequency descending, assign ranks
Rewrite mine() to write the new JSON schema including rejected_combinations section
Keep _build_bbv_map unchanged
Remove RejectedPair dataclass (unused from previous iteration)
validation_criteria: mine() writes JSON with new schema (topology, edges, examples fields)
mine() on float_chain_2.json fixture produces at least one pattern
mine() on fan_in_3node.json fixture produces a pattern with 2-layer topology
Patterns sorted by total_frequency descending with rank field
examples field contains concrete instruction instances per BB
rejected_combinations section is present in output

tasks.title: Rewrite test_miner.py: end-to-end mining tests
tasks.description: Rewrite miner tests to use the new subgraph-based approach and new output schema.
steps: Rewrite TestEnumerateChains → TestSubgraphMining with tests for subgraph-based flow
Write test_single_dfg_produces_patterns: float_chain_2.json → 1 pattern with size=2
Write test_fan_in_aggregation: fan_in_3node.json → pattern with 3 nodes, 2-layer topology
Write test_cross_bb_aggregation: two BBs with same pattern → merged frequencies
Write test_bb_not_in_hotspot: BB without BBV entry → frequency=0
Write test_ranked_by_frequency: patterns sorted descending
Write test_top_n_filtering: --top limits output
Rewrite TestMineOutput: verify new JSON schema fields (topology, edges, examples)
Write test_mine_empty_directory: empty dir → 0 patterns
validation_criteria: All tests pass with pytest
No test references chain_registers or the old Pattern model
Output schema assertions check for topology, edges, and examples fields
mine() on real DFG data (output/dfg/json/) produces non-zero patterns

tasks.title: Modify constraints.py: adapt to subgraph input
tasks.description: Adapt ConstraintChecker to accept subgraph patterns (with edges and topology fields) instead of linear chain_registers. Add new constraint options.
steps: Update check(pattern) to accept pattern dict with 'edges' field (list of {src_opcode, dst_opcode, src_role, dst_role})
Rewrite _check_operand_format: derive internal registers from edges, external registers from non-edge operands
Add new constraint 'no_control_flow' (hard, default enabled): check if any opcode is in _CONTROL_FLOW_MNEMONICS
Add new constraint 'max_nodes' (hard, default 8): check pattern size <= max_nodes
Add new constraint 'must_be_single_consumer' (hard, default disabled): check that only one node has no out-edges in the subgraph
Update ConstraintConfig.ALL_CONSTRAINTS with the three new entries
Keep existing per-instruction checks (encoding_32bit, no_load_store, etc.) unchanged
too_many_destinations/sources: continue using union of all flows (already works for subgraphs)
validation_criteria: check() accepts pattern dict with 'edges' field and produces correct Verdict
no_control_flow constraint flags patterns containing branch/jump instructions
max_nodes constraint rejects patterns exceeding the limit
operand_format correctly identifies internal vs external registers from subgraph edges
Existing constraints (encoding_32bit, datatype_encoding_space) still work
--list-constraints shows the three new constraints

tasks.title: Modify scorer.py: adapt to subgraph input
tasks.description: Adapt Scorer to work with subgraph patterns. Change tight_score to use edge/node ratio instead of linear chain density.
steps: Update score_pattern() to read 'edges' and 'size' from pattern dict instead of 'chain_registers' and 'opcodes' length
Rewrite _tight_score: use len(edges) / size as density metric, remove hardcoded _CHAIN_FACTOR dict
Update _freq_score and _hw_score: no logic changes needed
Update the output dict: replace chain_registers with edges in the 'pattern' section
Update tightness output: replace raw_density and chain_factor with edge_count, node_count, and density
Update score() CLI helper: handle new catalog schema (patterns list with edges/topology fields)
validation_criteria: score_pattern() works with pattern dict containing 'edges' and 'size' fields
tight_score uses edge_count / node_count ratio
Output dict contains 'edges' instead of 'chain_registers'
Infeasible patterns still score 0.0
Integration test passes with new schema

tasks.title: Modify __main__.py: add --max-nodes CLI arg
tasks.description: Add --max-nodes parameter to the discover command and pass it through to the miner.
steps: Add --max-nodes argument to argparse (type=int, default=8) for the discover command
Pass max_nodes to mine() function
Update mine() signature to accept max_nodes parameter
Pass max_nodes through to subgraph.enumerate_subgraphs()
validation_criteria: --max-nodes 3 limits subgraph enumeration to 3 nodes
--max-nodes defaults to 8
Invalid value (0 or negative) produces error message
python -m tools.fusion discover --help shows --max-nodes in usage

tasks.title: Modify test_integration.py: update scoring pipeline tests
tasks.description: Update the integration tests to use the new subgraph-based pattern schema in the scoring pipeline.
steps: Update TestScoringPipeline.setUp catalog to use new schema (replace chain_registers with edges/topology)
Update test_full_pipeline assertions to check new output fields
Update test_feasibility_only_mode for new schema
Keep TestEncodingMetadataAvailable tests unchanged (they test ISARegistry directly)
validation_criteria: All integration tests pass
Catalog uses new schema with edges and topology fields
Candidate output contains new tightness fields (edge_count, node_count, density)

tasks.title: End-to-end validation: run miner on real DFG data
tasks.description: Run the full pipeline (discover + score) on the actual YOLO DFG data in output/dfg/json/ and verify meaningful patterns are discovered.
steps: Run: python3 -m tools.fusion discover --dfg-dir output/dfg/json --report output/hotspot.json --output output/fusion_patterns.json --no-agent
Verify fusion_patterns.json contains non-zero pattern_count
Verify at least one pattern has size >= 3 (flw + flw + fmadd.s or similar)
Verify topology field shows correct layer structure
Verify edges field contains correct role pairs
Run: python3 -m tools.fusion score --catalog output/fusion_patterns.json --output output/fusion_candidates.json
Verify fusion_candidates.json contains scored candidates
Run full test suite: python3 -m pytest tools/fusion/tests/ -v
validation_criteria: pattern_count > 0 in fusion_patterns.json
At least one pattern with size >= 3
Topology has at least 2 layers for multi-node patterns
candidate_count > 0 in fusion_candidates.json
All unit tests pass (pytest tools/fusion/tests/ -v)
